revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Irou-jinja]][[ja:石室神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石室神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Irō Shrine | native_name = 石室神社 | image = Iroujinja.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Irō Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.6025|138.843333}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Irō Shrine'''|石室神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** P155: {{ill|Q134893135|ltq=Q134893135|qid=Q134893135|1=Hayatamawakenomikoto Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134893133|ltq=Q134893133|qid=Q134893133|1=Sutou Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Minamiizu|lt=Minamiizu|arz|ميناميزو|ceb|Minamiizu Chō|en|Minamiizu|es|Minamiizu|fa|مینامی‌ایزو|fr|Minamiizu|id|Minamiizu, Shizuoka|it|Minamiizu|ja|南伊豆町|ko|미나미이즈정|ms|Minamiizu, Shizuoka|pl|Minamiizu|ru|Минамиидзу|sv|Minamiizu|tl|Minamiizu, Shizuoka|tr|Minamiizu|uk|Мінамі-Ідзу|vi|Minamiizu, Shizuoka|zh_yue|南伊豆|zh|南伊豆町|qid=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html * {{ill|Q860928|ltq=Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} * {{ill|Q961511|ltq=Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.6025|138.843333}} == image (P18) == * Iroujinja.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181579 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917285|ltq=Q134917285|qid=Q134917285|1=Unranked shrines}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いしむろじんじゃ * いろうじんじゃ * イハレ- == postal code (P281) == * 415-0156https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8150686 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3080105004530https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Commons category (P373) == * Irou-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039641|ltq=Q135039641|qid=Q135039641|1=Ihare- Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県賀茂郡南伊豆町石廊崎125', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11586512|ltq=Q11586512|qid=Q11586512|1=Cape Irōzaki}} {{commons category|Category:Irou-jinja}} [[JA:石室神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11585503}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Irō Shrine |画像=[[画像:iroujinja.jpg|250px]]
Shrine building standing atop a sheer cliff on top of sail masts
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|36|9|N|138|50|36|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|qid=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}{{ill|lt=Irōzaki|Q11586512|qid=Q11586512|1=Cape Irōzaki}}125 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 36 | 緯度秒 = 9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 50 | 経度秒 = 36 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 | 地図 = Japan Shizuoka | ラベル位置 = left |祭神=Iware-no-Mikoto |社格=(trad.) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, unranked shrine |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|qid=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|qid=Q23496|1=701}}) |例祭= |神事=|}} '''Irō Shrine''' (いろうじんじゃ, いしむろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Irōzaki in {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|qid=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, near the tip of {{ill|lt=Irōzaki|Q11586512|qid=Q11586512|1=Cape Irōzaki}} at the southernmost end of the {{ill|lt=Izu Peninsula|Q822822|qid=Q822822|1=Izu Peninsula}}. It is also called '''Irō Gongen''' (いろうごんげん) or '''Irōzaki Gongen''' (いろうざきごんげん). It was formerly also said to be called '''Iware-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いはれのみことじんじゃ). The enshrined deity is now generally said to be Iware-no-Mikoto, but as noted below, this is not entirely certain. It is identified with “Iwarai no Myōjin, Junior Fourth Lower” in the {{ill|lt=Izu Province shrine-rank register|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu Province shrine-rank register}}. The shrine building is constructed above sea caves on top of mast-like pillars and is counted as one of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seven Wonders of Izu|1=UNKNOWN}}. About 50 meters away at the farthest tip of the cape is '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ), but in practice the two are often treated as one {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}, so both are described here together. === History and legends of Irō Shrine === ===History=== There are multiple theories about its origin and founding date, and nothing is fully certain. According to shrine tradition, a hall was first built in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|qid=Q23496|1=701}} (Taihō 1, reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|qid=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}). At first it housed a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kannon|1=UNKNOWN}} image and {{ill|lt=Tendō|Q13578873|qid=Q13578873|lt=Dairokutenjin|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven}}, and later {{ill|lt=En no Ozunu|Q198946|qid=Q198946|1=En no Gyōja}} received an oracle and enshrined Iware-no-Mikoto. On the other hand, according to the “Origin Record of Irōzan Kongōin” (displayed in the worship hall), when En no Ozunu was exiled to {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|qid=Q1070580|1=Izu Ōshima}} (from Monmu 3, {{ill|lt=699|Q23493|qid=Q23493|1=699}}, to Monmu 5, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|qid=Q23496|1=701}}), he reached this place by the divine power of {{ill|lt=Ekādaśamukha|Q11404731|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon of Fearlessness|1=Ekādaśamukha}}. Also, when a great earthquake occurred in Monmu 4 ({{ill|lt=700|Q23494|qid=Q23494|1=700}}), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=dragon|1=UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=white bird|1=UNKNOWN}} appeared and protected this place. A villager dreamed that a treasure hall rose from the sea and settled in a cave midway up the cape; when he went to look, a treasure hall had indeed appeared with {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|qid=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}} enshrined inside. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|qid=Q23561|1=729}}–{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|qid=Q23624|1=749}}), {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|qid=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} is said to have enshrined Dairokutenjin. Another influential theory says that {{ill|lt=Yuzuki no Kimi|Q11066250|qid=Q11066250|1=Yuzuki no Kimi}}, said to be a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Qin Shi Huang|Q7192|qid=Q7192|1=Qin Shi Huang}} of {{ill|lt=Qin|Q34756|qid=Q34756|1=Qin (state)}} who naturalized in Japan (with variant accounts), came to be called Monoi Mina-no-Mikoto, and that this shrine was established by the {{ill|lt=Hata clan|Q987290|qid=Q987290|1=Hata clan}}, who claimed descent from him, to worship that deity. However, whether this Monoi Mina-no-Mikoto is connected to {{ill|lt=Monoi Mina Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|lt=Monoi Mina-no-Mikoto|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}} enshrined on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|qid=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in former {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} remains unclear. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records an “Iware-no-Mikoto Shrine” in Kamo District, Izu Province. It is generally regarded as this shrine, though another view says this association was attached in later ages. Afterward it attracted devotion as Kongōzan Ishimuro Gongen under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it is said to have received a donation of two hyō of rice from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|1=UNKNOWN}} through the {{ill|lt=Nirayama daikansho|Q11664099|qid=Q11664099|1=Nirayama Daikansho}}. It is also said that the legend of “{{ill|lt=The mast of Irōzaki Gongen|Q11586514|qid=Q11586514|1=The mast of Irōzaki Gongen}},” associated with this shrine as one of the {{ill|lt=Seven Wonders of Izu|Q11380538|qid=Q11380538|1=Seven Mysteries of Izu}}, arose in this period. With the early-{{ill|lt=Meiji period|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji}} {{ill|lt=separation of kami and buddhas|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it came to be called Irō Shrine. The present shrine building was rebuilt in Meiji 34 ({{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|qid=Q2035|1=1901}}). It is famed as a deity of maritime safety, business prosperity, and academic success, and as a scenic spot at the tip of the {{ill|lt=Izu Peninsula|Q822822|qid=Q822822|1=Izu Peninsula}}. ===Legend=== {{main|石廊崎権現の帆柱}} === Origin and legend of Kumano Shrine === A village headman’s daughter named O-Shizu from Nagatsuro near {{ill|lt=Irōzaki|Q11586512|qid=Q11586512|1=Cape Irōzaki}} fell in love with a fisherman named Kōkichi. Because their different status made the relationship unacceptable, Kōkichi was exiled to {{ill|lt=Mikomoto Island|Q11589438|qid=Q11589438|1=Mikomoto Island}}. Unable to forget him, O-Shizu lit a fire every night at the tip of Irōzaki and confirmed their love with Kōkichi on Mikomoto Island. One night, worried because she could not see the fire on Mikomoto Island, O-Shizu put out in a small boat, but strong winds raised high waves and she could not advance. She prayed single-mindedly to the gods. By that grace she drifted to Mikomoto Island, and the two safely reunited and were joined. Their parents eventually accepted them, and they are said to have lived happily ever after. At the place where O-Shizu lit her fire, a small shrine to {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|1=Kumano Gongen}} was enshrined, and it came to be known as a deity of matchmaking. After the early-Meiji separation of kami and buddhas, it came to be called Kumano Shrine. === References === *Izu Studies Research Society, Committee for Publishing the Izu Encyclopedia (eds.), ''Izu Daijiten'', Hagoromo Publishing, 2010. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.town.minamiizu.shizuoka.jp/docs/2013031200453/ Guide to Irōzaki (Irō Shrine and Kumano Shrine) by Minamiizu Town] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Irō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Irō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Minamiizu]] [[Category:History of Minamiizu]] [[Category:静岡県の旧無格社]]
